title: CopilotAnalyzerService — periodic scheduler + LLM + dedup + expiry
predecessor: M33-T3 shipped 2026-04-16, commit c6b40ae (CopilotEventWindow + 8 unit tests; 1052 → 1060)
goal: The headline task of M33. Build the periodic CopilotAnalyzerService that drives the whole Copilot feature — it schedules a tick per company, snapshots the T3 CopilotEventWindow, runs the LLM via the pause-aware providerRouter.complete wrapper with actor='system-copilot' (T2 identity), parses the Zod-validated InsightDraftSchema output, dedups + persists via T1's CopilotInsightsRepo.upsertWithDedup, emits copilot.insight / copilot.analyzed / copilot.expired bus events per the Phase 5 §8.6 catalog, and handles errors / cancellation / budget exhaustion. Mirrors AgenticLoopService structure (M31) — pause-aware poll wrapper, AbortController per run, canceled-status coercion, runs-table row with kind='copilot' (requires migration 0012 extending runs.kind enum). Supplementary event-triggered runs via 30s-debounced triggerAnalysis(companyId, reason) for meeting.ended / ticket.closed / goal.progressChanged / agentic.failed with reason budget_exhausted.
acceptance: NEW apps/desktop/src/main/db/migrations/0012_copilot_runs_kind.sql — extends runs.kind enum to accept 'copilot'. SQLite enums are CHECK-constraint shaped; this migration should drop + recreate the CHECK with the new member, per the M31 T4 migration pattern for runs.kind

NEW packages/intelligence/src/copilot/analyzer.ts (or apps/desktop/src/main/services/copilot-analyzer-service.ts — check plan doc §T4 for canonical placement; default to apps/desktop mirroring AgenticLoopService) — CopilotAnalyzerService with API { start(companyId), stop(companyId), tick(companyId, opts?), getLastAnalysisAt(companyId): number | null, restart(companyId) }

buildAnalysisPrompt(window, companyContext, categories): string — deterministic, ≤2000 tokens, reads CopilotEventWindow.snapshot + recent CopilotInsightsRepo.listActive (to avoid re-proposing already-active insights) + company metadata (name, active projects, open tickets summary)

Zod InsightDraftSchema parses LLM output: Array<{ category: CopilotCategory, severity: CopilotSeverity, title: string, body: string, relatedEntities?: {...}, expiresInHours?: number (clamped server-side 1–168) }>. One-shot nudge retry on malformed JSON (structured 'please return JSON matching schema X'); second failure skips cycle + emits copilot.analyzed with insightsGenerated=0, reason='malformed_output'

Pause-aware providerRouter.complete wrapper (actor: 'system-copilot') — same poll-isCompanyPaused pattern M31 AgenticLoopService uses (250ms prod, 2ms test); AbortController-driven cancel with canceled-status coercion on the runs row
Dedup via CopilotInsightsRepo.upsertWithDedup — emits 'copilot.insight' ONLY for newly-inserted rows, never for merges (count merge vs insert and reflect in copilot.analyzed payload)

Expiry sweep per cycle — calls copilotInsightsRepo.expireStale(now) BEFORE building the prompt (so expired insights don't appear in listActive context); emits 'copilot.expired' per row that transitioned expired

Event-triggered supplementary ticks — CopilotEventWindow bus subscription extended (or a sibling subscriber) with 30s-debounced triggerAnalysis(companyId, reason) for four signal types: meeting.ended, ticket.closed, goal.progressChanged, agentic.failed with payload reason='budget_exhausted'. Per-company debounce timer (Map<companyId, Timer>). Scheduled tick and event-triggered tick share the same tick() code path

Bus events emitted with canonical EventType entries (requires adding 'copilot.insight' | 'copilot.analyzed' | 'copilot.expired' to packages/shared-types/src/events.ts EventType union + payload interfaces): CopilotInsightPayload, CopilotAnalyzedPayload, CopilotExpiredPayload

Runs-table row per tick with kind='copilot', actor='system-copilot' employeeId, provider + model + tokens + costUsd recorded — telemetry tab already aggregates runs by kind, so copilot runs appear in Telemetry → Cost without UI changes

+14 unit tests net (1060 → 1074 target): buildAnalysisPrompt determinism (2), Zod schema happy-path + malformed retry (3), pause-aware dispatch (2), dedup integration (2), expiry cycle (2), event-triggered debounce (2), AbortController cancel (1)
Typecheck clean across all 6 workspace packages
Atomic commit: feat(m33): M33 T4 — copilot analyzer service + pause-aware scheduler
Ledger commit: chore(loki): M33 T4 — commit ledger (<sha>)
filesTouched: apps/desktop/src/main/db/schema.ts — update runs.kind TypeScript type union to include 'copilot'

packages/shared-types/src/events.ts — add 'copilot.insight' | 'copilot.analyzed' | 'copilot.expired' to EventType; export CopilotInsightPayload + CopilotAnalyzedPayload + CopilotExpiredPayload interfaces

apps/desktop/src/main/services/copilot-analyzer-service.ts (NEW) — CopilotAnalyzerService factory, tick() runner, buildAnalysisPrompt helper, InsightDraftSchema zod, dedup+expire+emit flow, AbortController/pause wrapper

apps/desktop/src/main/services/copilot-event-window.ts (T3 file — EXTEND with triggerAnalysis debouncer? OR add a sibling 'copilot-event-trigger.ts' to keep T3 untouched — preferred to keep T3 pure; put 30s debounce + 4 signal types in a new file that imports the window + the analyzer)

apps/desktop/src/main/services/copilot-event-trigger.ts (NEW, preferred over extending T3) — per-company 30s debounced triggerAnalysis dispatcher; subscribes to the bus and calls analyzer.tick(companyId, { reason }) with debounce

apps/desktop/src/main/index.ts — composition root wiring: instantiate CopilotAnalyzerService with deps (copilotEventWindow, copilotInsightsRepo, providerRouter, orchestrator for pause-gate, employeesRepo for system-copilot lookup, runsRepo, bus); instantiate CopilotEventTrigger with (bus, analyzer); wire will-quit shutdown (stop analyzer BEFORE orchestrator drain, same rationale as ragIndexer)
notes: This is the largest task in M33 and carries the biggest design surface. Before the first edit: re-read Phase 5 §8.4 (analyzer prompt contract), §8.5 (CopilotEventWindow consumer), §8.6 (bus event catalog), §8.7 (dedup + expiry semantics). Plan doc T4 §.

Mirror AgenticLoopService structure. M31 T4 (agentic-loop-service.ts) is the canonical reference for the pause-aware wrapper, AbortController-driven cancel, canceled-status coercion on the runs row, and runs-table integration. Read that file first and match its shapes.

Pause-aware providerRouter.complete wrapper — reuse M31 constants POLL_INTERVAL_MS_PROD = 250 and POLL_INTERVAL_MS_TEST = 2. The poll checks `orchestrator.isCompanyPaused(companyId)` between every provider completion; if paused mid-stream, the cycle is a no-op (emits copilot.analyzed with reason='company_paused').

Event-triggered debounce design: `Map<companyId, { timer: NodeJS.Timeout, reason: string }>` — if a second trigger fires for the same company before the 30s timer elapses, RESET the timer (not cumulative — debounce not throttle). Debounce fires the most-recent reason. Test this explicitly.

SPLIT the T3 window vs the T4 trigger into separate files. Do NOT extend copilot-event-window.ts with analyzer coupling — the window is a pure accumulator and stays that way. The trigger is its own subscriber that imports both the window + the analyzer. This preserves T3's test isolation and keeps dependency direction acyclic.

Zod InsightDraftSchema: server-side clamp expiresInHours to [1, 168] (1 hour to 1 week); LLM proposals outside that range get clipped with a log warning, NOT rejected. Category + severity are enum-validated strictly — rejection triggers the nudge retry.

Nudge retry pattern (mirror M31 T1 one-shot parser recovery): on first Zod failure, re-issue the prompt with a structured 'the previous output did not match schema X; please return a valid JSON array matching this shape:' preamble. If second attempt also fails, emit copilot.analyzed with insightsGenerated=0, reason='malformed_output' and consume the budget (cycle counts as spent).

Dedup integration via CopilotInsightsRepo.upsertWithDedup (T1 shipped): the repo returns { inserted: boolean, insightId: string } per draft. Emit copilot.insight ONLY when inserted=true; skip emission on merge. Reflect in copilot.analyzed payload as insightsGenerated (inserted count) vs insightsProposed (total drafts).

Expiry sweep timing: call copilotInsightsRepo.expireStale(now) at the TOP of tick() BEFORE buildAnalysisPrompt. This prevents expired insights from contaminating the prompt's 'already-active' context. expireStale returns the expired row count — emit one copilot.expired per expired row (not an aggregated event).

Migration 0012 shape — SQLite CHECK constraints cannot be altered in place; the drizzle-kit generator creates a temp-table swap pattern. Run `pnpm -F @team-x/desktop exec drizzle-kit generate --name copilot_runs_kind` and REVIEW the generated SQL before applying — the temp-table swap must preserve row count + column order. M32 T4 did the same dance for runs.kind with 'agentic'.

packages/shared-types/src/events.ts — adding to EventType is a breaking-change candidate for any exhaustive switch statement. Before committing, grep for 'switch.*event.type' and '(.type)' exhaustive uses in the renderer (AuditView, TimelineView) and confirm the new values have UI-side handling or safe-default fall-through.

runs-table copilot rows — telemetry-core calcCostUsd is already generic over provider + model; no telemetry package changes needed. TelemetryView will render copilot runs in the existing 'kind' column alongside agentic/work. If a cosmetic label is needed, that's an M33 T7 (settings + UI) or M34 concern, not T4.

ABI rebuild dance BEFORE first vitest run at T4: `cd node_modules/.pnpm/better-sqlite3@11.10.0/node_modules/better-sqlite3 && npx node-gyp rebuild --release` + same for keytar. Migration 0012 touches the DB schema; the dev DB at %APPDATA%/Team-X/team-x/team-x.sqlite may need to be nuked if the CHECK-constraint swap can't be applied to an existing DB (typical for SQLite enum extensions). Document the nuke-path in the commit body if applied.

Invariant preservation: #2 (orchestrator is the only scheduler) — the analyzer's setInterval is a secondary scheduler but it OBSERVES the orchestrator's pause state (isCompanyPaused) and does not dispatch through the orchestrator's work queue; it goes direct to providerRouter.complete. This is consistent with M31 AgenticLoopService precedent. Document in §2 design notes.

Invariant #7 (zero phone-home) — the analyzer makes LLM calls to the user-configured provider (possibly cloud if Rocky chose Anthropic/OpenAI/etc.); that is NOT phone-home, it's the user's explicit provider choice. No anonymous metrics leave the machine.

Invariant #11 (IPC mutations emit bus events) — copilot.analyzed, copilot.insight, copilot.expired are all emitted on the bus inside the same code path that writes to copilot_insights + runs. Tests must verify both the DB write AND the bus emit via FakeBus capture.

T4 is the right task for a subagent pass on the prompt-builder scaffolding. Spawn an Explore subagent to read Phase 5 §8.4 + M31 AgenticLoopService + M29 RAG retriever for context, then write buildAnalysisPrompt as a deterministic pure function. Main session does the service wiring, migration, tests.
